dialogues.text: Adaptation is a question. Can we change and grow, or are we doomed to remain stuck in a prison of our own making? Charlie Kaufman is in trouble. Even though his first feature film, Being John Malkovich, is currently shooting, no one knows or even cares that he's the one who wrote it. Even worse, his agent convinced him to write the screenplay for a feature film version of Susan Orlean's huge nonfiction hit the Orchid Thief, a story about flowers and John Laroche, the iconoclastic and potentially psychotic man who hunts these rare plants in Florida's Faxahatchee State Preserve. Paralyzed with self doubt in his every interaction, Charlie is torn between his desire to avoid formulaic film tropes in this elusive script and his deepening jealousy over his twin Brother Donald's success writing a cliche drench hit thriller as he has drawn deeper into obsession. Following Orlean and Laroche into the swamp of their own drug fueled romance. And faced with his and Donald's own mortality, Charlie will have to decide for himself whether to wilt under pressure or if he is capable of adaptation.
